description: Baseline C1/C3 conflict rates across full SWARM-LANES history at S428
method: Parse SWARM-LANES.md + SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md final-state per lane; classify C3 (stale open) and C1 (abandoned with 'superseded' keyword indicating duplicate work)

c3_orphaning.note: No open or stale lanes at measurement time. All lanes are MERGED or ABANDONED.

c1_duplicate_work.method_note: Count of ABANDONED lanes with 'superseded' in lane notes (broad proxy for duplicate work)
c1_duplicate_work.caveat: Includes both true C1 duplicates and lanes abandoned due to concurrent preemption (L-526 commit-by-proxy pattern). True C1 rate likely lower; concurrent preemption is structural at N>=5 sessions.
c1_duplicate_work.admin_closure_note: 293/345 abandoned lanes have admin-closure patterns (cycle complete, session end, preempted)

analysis.merge_rate_vs_s189: 70.7% vs 94.6% — 24pp decline due to high-concurrency abandonment (L-526)
analysis.abandon_rate_vs_s189: 29.2% vs 4.5% — 6.5x increase; structural at N>=5 concurrent sessions
analysis.c1_rate_vs_s189: 6.6% vs 1.3% — increase driven by concurrency preemption (broad keyword proxy)
analysis.swarm_scale_effect: At 1181 lanes and N>=10 concurrency, commit-by-proxy is the default pattern

interpretations: C3 (stale-open) rate is 0% — better than S189. Lane hygiene on open lanes is excellent.
C1 proxy rate increased to 6.6% but is inflated by concurrency preemption (not true duplicate work).
The 'superseded' keyword is a broad proxy; true intentional C1 duplicates likely still ~1-3%.
High abandonment rate (29.2%) is a structural consequence of extreme concurrency (L-526), not hygiene failure.
Administrative closures dominate abandoned lanes: 85% are expected preemptions, not waste.
